questions.incorrect_feedback: Take another look! One of these sounds like chatting with a buddy, not a school essay. Which sounds too casual and relaxed?
questions.reveal_feedback: The answer is "Yeah, it's super cool." That sounds too casual, like talking to a friend, so it does not fit an essay.

questions.prompt: Which word is a stronger way to say **big**?
questions.hint: Enormous means very, very big!
questions.correct_feedback: Perfect! Enormous is a strong word that means really, really big. Strong words make your writing pop. Nice!
questions.incorrect_feedback: Hmm, we want ONE strong word that means very big, not words like kinda or medium. Which word means super big?
questions.reveal_feedback: The answer is enormous. It is a strong word that means really, really big, which is a powerful way to say big.

questions.prompt: You are writing a note to thank your school principal for the brand-new library. Which sentence sounds the most polite and formal?
choices: We really appreciate the wonderful new library.
You got us a great library, thanks a lot!
Thanks for the awesome new books!
The new library is super cool, thanks!
questions.correct: We really appreciate the wonderful new library.
questions.hint: A principal is someone you should speak to respectfully. Look for the sentence with no slang words like 'awesome,' 'super cool,' or 'a lot.'
questions.correct_feedback: Great! Writing to a principal should sound polite and grown-up. This one uses kind, formal words. Well done!
questions.incorrect_feedback: Good try! A note to your principal should sound polite and proper, not like chatting. Which sentence sounds the most formal?
questions.reveal_feedback: The answer is "We really appreciate the wonderful new library." It sounds the most polite and formal for a principal.

questions.prompt: Your brother is only 4 years old and asks what the word 'enormous' means. Which is the BEST way to explain it so he understands?
questions.hint: Match your words to your listener. A 4-year-old only knows simple words, so pick the explanation he could actually understand.
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! For a 4-year-old, we use easy words. "It means really, really big" is simple and clear. Perfect choice!
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! A 4-year-old needs really simple words, not big fancy ones. Which one uses the easiest words to understand?
questions.reveal_feedback: The answer is "It means really, really big." For a 4-year-old, simple, easy words like these are the clearest way to explain.

questions.prompt: You are writing a science report for class, which should sound formal. Which sentence fits a report best?
choices: So bats are, like, mammals and not birds!
Bats are not birds; they are mammals.
questions.correct: Bats are not birds; they are mammals.
questions.hint: Formal writing avoids shortened words (contractions) and chatty words like 'so' and 'like.' Find the sentence that spells everything out fully.
questions.correct_feedback: Great! A report should sound formal. "Bats are not birds; they are mammals" uses full, proper words. Nice work!
questions.incorrect_feedback: Take another look! A science report should sound proper, not use words like ain't or like. Which sentence sounds the most formal?
questions.reveal_feedback: The answer is "Bats are not birds; they are mammals." It uses full, proper words, so it fits a formal report best.

questions.prompt: The excited puppy raced to the door the second it heard us coming home. Which word shows its movement most precisely?
questions.hint: The puppy is excited and fast. Pick the word that shows the MOST speed and energy, not just any kind of moving.
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! The puppy raced fast to the door. Dashed means to move very quickly, so it shows the action best. Great pick!
questions.incorrect_feedback: Close one! The excited puppy RACED to the door. Which word shows moving super fast, not just plain moving or walking?
questions.reveal_feedback: The best word is dashed. It means to move very quickly, which matches the excited puppy racing to the door.

questions.prompt: The winter wind was so strong and cold it hurt your face. Which word describes that wind most precisely?
questions.hint: Think about which word matches a wind that is sharp and painful, not just any wind. Some choices sound pleasant or too plain.
questions.correct_feedback: Great! The wind was so cold it hurt your face. Biting means so cold and sharp it stings. That fits perfectly!
questions.incorrect_feedback: Hmm, this wind was so cold and sharp it HURT your face. Which word shows wind that stings, not just windy or nice?
questions.reveal_feedback: The best word is biting. It means so cold and sharp it stings, which matches wind that hurt your face.
